perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by German law. All disputes between signed parties shall fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts who have jurisdiction for the district in which the German Partner has its registered office. The German Partner’s registered offi ce is located in Fr iedrichstraß e 10, 70174 Stuttgart, Germany.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. Competition The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either Party from developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or providing products or services to others who compete with the other Party; as long as those results have not become from a breach of this Agreement.

4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by German law. All disputes between signed parties shall fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts who have jurisdiction for the district in which the German Partner has its registered office. The German Partner’s registered offi ce is located in Fr iedrichstraß e 10, 70174 Stuttgart, Germany.
perturbation.explanation: The placement of the 'Applicable Law and Jurisdiction' clause under the 'Competition' section is misleading. These clauses are standard, and should be under the 'Miscellaneous' section, where they are normally expected. Relocating it makes it less prominent and easily overlooked. This is intended to make it harder for a party unfamiliar with German Law to quickly understand their obligations under German law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The relocated clause risks being overlooked or misinterpreted. This is because German law requires any ambiguities in standard contract terms to be interpreted against the drafter of the contract. By burying the jurisdiction clause, it may lead to a dispute over jurisdiction if the clause is ambiguous or unclear. Placing a clause determining jurisdiction in an unexpected section can be argued to make it unclear, thus weakening its enforceability under German Law.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the relocation of the 'Applicable Law and Jurisdiction' clause, a standard clause typically found in the 'Miscellaneous' section, to the 'Competition' section is misleading. This is intended to obscure the clause and make it less prominent, potentially hindering a party unfamiliar with German law from understanding their obligations. The law explanation supports this by stating that placing the jurisdiction clause in an unexpected section can make it unclear, weakening its enforceability under German Law. The structural flaw perturbation definition states that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. While the scraped law snippets do not directly address this issue of clause placement, the combination of the perturbation explanation, law explanation, the changed text and the definition for structural flaw contradictions sufficiently support that a legal contradiction exists as the changed text attempts to obscure the governing law, making it harder to interpret.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. Competition The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either Party from developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or providing products or services to others who compete with the other Party; as long as those results have not become from a breach of this Agreement.

3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduce d or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreemen t; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the clause concerning the return or destruction of confidential information to the 'Competition' section will not alert the recipient to their duty to return or destroy all confidential information upon termination, leading to a breach of contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: German law prohibits the unauthorized use and disclosure of trade secrets (Geschäftsgeheimnisse). Moving the clause about returning/destroying confidential information to an unexpected section (Competition) increases the likelihood that the Receiving Party might overlook this obligation, potentially leading to unauthorized use or disclosure of the confidential information after the agreement terminates. This can violate § 17 of the UWG if the information qualifies as a trade secret.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that relocating the clause concerning the return or destruction of confidential information could lead to a breach of contract. The Law Explanation states this could violate § 17 of the UWG if the information qualifies as a trade secret because the Receiving Party might overlook this obligation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that § 17 UWG was repealed. However, the structural flaw of moving the return of confidential information clause to an unexpected location within the contract increases the risk that the receiving party will not be aware of their obligations, which impacts enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3.10 The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and tha t the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text:
4.5 Communications Any notices or communications required between the Parties shall be delivered by hand, e - mail, or mailed by registered mail to the address of the other Party as indicated above. Any subsequent modification of a Party’s address should be reasonably communic ated in advance to the effect of this Agreement.

3.10 The Parties agree that the Disclosing Party will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and tha t the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such a breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The clause outlining remedies for breach of confidentiality, specifically injunctive relief and damages, is moved to a section on communications, making it harder to find and recognize as a critical provision for protecting confidential information. The expectation is not there for this type of clause to be under Communications.
perturbation.law_explanation: Relocating the clause may make it more difficult for the Disclosing Party to quickly demonstrate to a German court that irreparable harm is likely to occur, as required by ZPO § 935. This provision allows for preliminary injunctions to prevent further damage. By obscuring the clause that acknowledges irreparable harm, the Disclosing Party might face greater difficulty in obtaining immediate injunctive relief, because that section is not near the Confidentiality section.

perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states the remedies for breach of confidentiality. By moving this clause to a general 'Communications' section, the changed text obscures the critical nature of these remedies, making it more difficult to quickly demonstrate irreparable harm in court, as the Law Explanation states referencing ZPO § 935. The Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines ZPO § 935 as relating to 'Einstweilige Verfügung bezüglich Streitgegenstand', which translates to 'Preliminary injunction regarding the subject of the dispute,' and states that it applies when 'zu besorgen ist, dass durch eine Veränderung des bestehenden Zustandes die Verwirklichung des Rechts einer Partei vereitelt oder wesentlich erschwert werden könnte,' which translates to 'there is reason to fear that a change in the existing state could frustrate or significantly impede the realization of a party's right.' By hiding the remedies in an unexpected section, the changed text undermines the ability to quickly and effectively obtain such an injunction, thus creating a legal contradiction based on Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: 3.9 In the event that the Receiving Party is asked to communicate the Confidential Information to any judicial, administrative, regulatory authority or similar or oblige d to reveal such information by mandatory law, it shall notify promptly the Disclosing Party of the terms of such disclosure and will collaborate to the extent practicable with the Disclosing Party in order to comply with the order and preserve the confide ntiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text:
4.3 Validity If any provisions of this Agreement are invalid or unenforceable, the validity of the remaining provisions shall not be affected. The Parties shall replace the invalid or unenforceable provision by a valid and enforceable provision that will meet the purpose of the invalid or unenforceable provision as closely as possible.

3.9 In the event that the Receiving Party is asked to communicate the Confidential Information to any judicial, administrative, regulatory authority or similar or oblige d to reveal such information by mandatory law, it shall notify promptly the Disclosing Party of the terms of such disclosure and will collaborate to the extent practicable with the Disclosing Party in order to comply with the order and preserve the confide ntiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The duty to notify and collaborate in case of mandatory disclosure to authorities is moved to the 'Validity' section, which usually deals with severability. This is intended to obfuscate the importance and process of handling legally mandated disclosures, increasing the risk of non-compliance and potential legal repercussions.
perturbation.law_explanation: The relocation of the clause detailing the duty to inform and collaborate in case of mandatory disclosure undermines the constitutional protection of academic and artistic freedom (GG Art. 5 III). If the Receiving Party does not give timely notice of required disclosures, the Disclosing Party loses the opportunity to argue against it, potentially leading to disclosures which jeopardize projects covered by Art. 5 III.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Schleswig-Holstein and Thuringia have achieved the unity and freedom of Germany in free self-determination. This Basic Law thus applies to the entire German people. table of contents I. Basic Rights table of contents Article 1 [Human dignity – Human rights – Legally binding force of basic rights] (1) Human dignity shall be inviolable. To respect and protect it shall be the duty of all state authority. (2) The German people therefore acknowledge inviolable and inalienable human rights as the basis of every community, of peace and of justice in the world. (3) The following basic rights shall bind the legislature, the executive and the judiciary as directly applicable law. table of contents Article 2 [Personal freedoms] (1) Every person shall have the right to free development of his personality insofar as he does not violate the rights of others or offend against the constitutional order or the moral law. (2) Every person shall have the right to life and physical integrity. Freedom of the person shall be inviolable. These rights may be interfered with only pursuant to a law. table of contents Article 3 [Equality before the law] (1) All persons shall be equal before the law. (2) Men and women shall have equal rights. The state shall promote the actual implementation of equal rights for women and men and take steps to eliminate existing disadvantages. (3) No person shall be favoured or disfavoured because of sex, parentage, race, language, homeland and origin, faith or religious or political opinions. No person shall be disfavoured because of disability. table of contents Article 4 [Freedom of faith and conscience] (1) Freedom of faith and of conscience and freedom to profess a religious or philosophical creed shall be inviolable. (2) The undisturbed practice of religion shall be guaranteed. (3) No person shall be compelled against his conscience to render military service involving the use of arms. Details shall be regulated by a federal law. table of contents Article 5 [Freedom of expression, arts and sciences] (1) Every person shall have the right freely to express and disseminate his opinions in speech, writing and pictures and to inform himself without hindrance from generally accessible sources. Freedom of the press and freedom of reporting by means of broadcasts and films shall be guaranteed. There shall be no censorship. (2) These rights shall find their limits in the provisions of general laws, in provisions for the protection of young persons and in the right to personal honour. (3) Arts and sciences, research and teaching shall be free. The freedom of teaching shall not release any person from allegiance to the constitution. table of contents Article 6 [Marriage – Family – Children] (1) Marriage and the family shall enjoy the special protection of the state. (2) The care and upbringing of children is the natural right of parents and a duty primarily incumbent upon them. The state shall watch over them in the performance of this duty. (3) Children may be separated from their families against the will of their parents or guardians only pursuant to a law and only if the parents or guardians fail in their duties or the children are otherwise in danger of serious neglect. (4) Every mother shall be entitled to the protection and care of the community. (5) Children born outside of marriage shall be provided by legislation with the same opportunities for physical and mental development and for their position in society as are enjoyed by those born within marriage. table of contents Article 7 [School system] (1) The entire school system shall be under the supervision of the state. (2) Parents and guardians shall have the right to decide whether children shall receive religious instruction. (3) Religious instruction shall form part of the regular curriculum in state schools, with the exception of non-denominational schools. Without prejudice to the state’s right of supervision, religious instruction shall be given in accordance wi
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of moving the mandatory disclosure clause to the 'Validity' section creates a legal contradiction. This relocation obfuscates the clause, making it less likely to be noticed and followed. The law explanation suggests this could undermine academic and artistic freedom (Art. 5 III) because delayed notification hampers the Disclosing Party's ability to argue against the disclosure. Scraped Law Snippet 1 guarantees freedom of expression, arts, and sciences (Art. 5), implying that the original clause was designed to protect this freedom by ensuring timely notification. The modification weakens this protection by making the clause less accessible, thus creating a direct and enforceable contradiction.
